prompts.proposer:
Call proposer_brief() FIRST. The tool output includes pattern_analyst_insights — use trend_champion, seasonality_champion, and insights.recommended_method_hint to bias your candidate selection toward non-mean methods. MUST propose at least 3 candidates including at least 1 non-baseline type. Return ONLY JSON per instructions. IMPORTANT: you MUST ONLY reference candidate names that appear in the tool output candidate_library; unknown names will hard-stop.
PATTERN ANALYST INSIGHTS: trend_champion='ARIMA', seasonality_champion='THETA', recommended_method='dba_combination'. Narrative: Analysis reveals significant model disagreement (rmse_spread_ratio=0.42) and high seasonality capture variance (0.56), with ARIMA best tracking trends while THETA excels at seasonal patterns. The true series alternates between up/down trends across folds, requiring flexible combination methods. Given both high disagreement and seasonality variance, DBA combination is optimal to leverage model diversity without overfitting to any single pattern.
prompts.skeptic:
Chame build_debate_packet_tool() PRIMEIRO (inputs via context). Depois retorne APENAS JSON (sem markdown) com add_names, remove_names, params_overrides, rationale, changes, when_good.
IMPORTANT: you MUST ONLY use candidate names from valid_candidate_names; unknown names hard-stop.
You may only remove/override candidates that are in current_candidate_names (or candidates you are adding).

raw.pattern_analyst:
{
  "trend_champion": "ARIMA",
  "seasonality_champion": "THETA",
  "overall_champion": "ONLY_FT_catboost",
  "horizon_specialists": {"early": "ONLY_DWT_rf", "late": "rf"},
  "tier1_models": ["ONLY_FT_catboost", "ONLY_FT_svr", "THETA", "FT_svr", "NaiveMovingAverage", "ETS", "ARIMA", "DWT_rf"],
  "tier2_models": ["ONLY_DWT_rf", "DWT_catboost", "svr", "CWT_svr", "DWT_svr", "ONLY_CWT_svr", "rf", "CWT_catboost"],
  "recommended_method_hint": "dba_combination",
  "recommended_weighting_basis": "mixed",
  "key_insights": {
    "rmse_spread_ratio": 0.4219,
    "high_disagreement": true,
    "high_seasonality_variance": true,
    "ytrue_trend_directions": ["down", "up", "down"]
  },
  "cot_narrative": "Analysis reveals significant model disagreement (rmse_spread_ratio=0.42) and high seasonality capture variance (0.56), with ARIMA best tracking trends while THETA excels at seasonal patterns. The true series alternates between up/down trends across folds, requiring flexible combination methods. Given both high disagreement and seasonality variance, DBA combination is optimal to leverage model diversity without overfitting to any single pattern."
}
raw.proposer:
{
  "selected_names": ["dba_combination", "best_per_horizon_by_validation", "topk_mean_per_horizon_k5", "inverse_rmse_weights_k5_sh0.35", "trimmed_mean_r0.25"],
  "params_overrides": {},
  "score_preset": "robust_smape",
  "force_debate": false,
  "debate_margin": 0.02,
  "rationale": "n_unique_winners=9 indicates high model diversity requiring per-horizon selection methods. relative_spread_mean=0.41 exceeds 0.25 threshold, favoring robust combination (dba_combination) and trimmed means. n_windows=3 is very low, so top_k=5 with shrinkage=0.35 provides stability. Pattern analyst recommends dba_combination for high disagreement and seasonality variance (0.56). RMSE spread ratio=0.42 suggests inverse_rmse_weights helps leverage model diversity. Score preset 'robust_smape' chosen because avg SMAPE=0.640 > 0.3 scale-sensitive series."
}
raw.skeptic: {"add_names": ["ade_dynamic_error_beta0.5_trim0.8"], "remove_names": [], "params_overrides": {}, "rationale": "Current set has 5 candidates with good diversity (2 baseline: dba_combination, trimmed_mean_r0.25; 2 selection: best_per_horizon_by_validation, topk_mean_per_horizon_k5; 1 weighted: inverse_rmse_weights_k5_sh0.35). No leakage issues detected (n_windows=3, all strategies safe). However, POCID leaderboard shows ade_dynamic_error candidates with POCID~60.6 vs current best dba_combination at 51.5. Adding ade_dynamic_error_beta0.5_trim0.8 improves POCID diversity without violating rules (no type has ≥4 candidates). No removals needed as no redundancy threshold exceeded.", "changes": ["Added ade_dynamic_error_beta0.5_trim0.8 from POCID leaderboard for improved POCID performance"], "when_good": ""}
raw.statistician: {"add_names": [], "remove_names": [], "params_overrides": {}, "rationale": "Current candidate set is well-diversified: dba_combination (handles phase shifts), best_per_horizon_by_validation (9 unique winners ≥3 threshold), inverse_rmse_weights_k5_sh0.35 (weighted for model quality variation), topk_mean_per_horizon_k5 (selection method), trimmed_mean_r0.25 (robust aggregation), ade_dynamic_error_beta0.5_trim0.8 (adaptive). High disagreement (relative_spread_mean=0.41≥0.25) is already addressed by DBA and weighted methods. n_windows=3 (low) suggests stronger regularization - current shrinkage=0.35 is appropriate. Stability flag shows rmse_std_over_rmse=0.265, slightly below 0.3 threshold. Universe leaderboards show top performers (trimmed_mean_r0.20/0.30, robust_median) are similar to existing candidates. No changes needed - set is already optimized.", "changes": [], "when_good": ""}

pattern_analyst.cot_narrative: Analysis reveals significant model disagreement (rmse_spread_ratio=0.42) and high seasonality capture variance (0.56), with ARIMA best tracking trends while THETA excels at seasonal patterns. The true series alternates between up/down trends across folds, requiring flexible combination methods. Given both high disagreement and seasonality variance, DBA combination is optimal to leverage model diversity without overfitting to any single pattern.

proposer.rationale: n_unique_winners=9 indicates high model diversity requiring per-horizon selection methods. relative_spread_mean=0.41 exceeds 0.25 threshold, favoring robust combination (dba_combination) and trimmed means. n_windows=3 is very low, so top_k=5 with shrinkage=0.35 provides stability. Pattern analyst recommends dba_combination for high disagreement and seasonality variance (0.56). RMSE spread ratio=0.42 suggests inverse_rmse_weights helps leverage model diversity. Score preset 'robust_smape' chosen because avg SMAPE=0.640 > 0.3 scale-sensitive series.

skeptic.rationale: Current set has 5 candidates with good diversity (2 baseline: dba_combination, trimmed_mean_r0.25; 2 selection: best_per_horizon_by_validation, topk_mean_per_horizon_k5; 1 weighted: inverse_rmse_weights_k5_sh0.35). No leakage issues detected (n_windows=3, all strategies safe). However, POCID leaderboard shows ade_dynamic_error candidates with POCID~60.6 vs current best dba_combination at 51.5. Adding ade_dynamic_error_beta0.5_trim0.8 improves POCID diversity without violating rules (no type has ≥4 candidates). No removals needed as no redundancy threshold exceeded.
changes: Added ade_dynamic_error_beta0.5_trim0.8 from POCID leaderboard for improved POCID performance

statistician.rationale: Current candidate set is well-diversified: dba_combination (handles phase shifts), best_per_horizon_by_validation (9 unique winners ≥3 threshold), inverse_rmse_weights_k5_sh0.35 (weighted for model quality variation), topk_mean_per_horizon_k5 (selection method), trimmed_mean_r0.25 (robust aggregation), ade_dynamic_error_beta0.5_trim0.8 (adaptive). High disagreement (relative_spread_mean=0.41≥0.25) is already addressed by DBA and weighted methods. n_windows=3 (low) suggests stronger regularization - current shrinkage=0.35 is appropriate. Stability flag shows rmse_std_over_rmse=0.265, slightly below 0.3 threshold. Universe leaderboards show top performers (trimmed_mean_r0.20/0.30, robust_median) are similar to existing candidates. No changes needed - set is already optimized.
